metadata.note: Iteration 2 tests only Eval 1 (recommendation logic). Evals 2-4 (hook behavior) were unchanged and remain at 100% pass from iteration 1.

expectations.text: Report contains all 5 required metric lines
Recommendation correctly uses cache delta (no false alarm for improving cache)
expectations.evidence: 'Cache hit rate improving (+11.4pp) but still below 85% target — keep going' — correctly condition 5, not stagnant condition 4

notes: Fix confirmed: condition 5 (improving+11.4pp) fires instead of false-alarm condition 4 (stagnant).

expectations.text: Report contains all 5 required metric lines
Recommendation correctly uses cache delta (no false alarm for improving cache)
expectations.evidence: 'Cache hit rate below target' fires even though cache improved +11.4pp — false alarm erodes user trust in the recommendation system

notes: Confirmed iteration-1 regression: cache recommendation ignores direction of change.
Pass rate improved from 80% (old_skill) to 100% (with_skill) on Eval 1 — the one affected assertion now passes.
New skill is also faster (51s vs 121s) and uses fewer tokens (25K vs 27K) — likely because the clearer priority-ordered table leads to less deliberation.

The fix eliminated a false alarm: the old skill told users with an improving cache rate that their cache was 'below target' with no acknowledgment of improvement. The new message 'improving (+11.4pp) but still below 85%' is accurate and actionable.
Evals 2-4 (hook behavior) unchanged — still 100% pass from iteration 1.
